Found 58 results discussing the use of Freund's complete adjuvant in animal models. Multiple studies confirm that Freund's complete adjuvant triggers severe local and systemic inflammation, often resulting in pannus formation and joint destructionhallmarks of rheumatoid-like arthritis. In particular, experiments show that injection of antigen-adjuvant complexes into joints leads to sustained autoimmunity and synovial hyperplasia mimicking human RA pathology. Some reports indicate that even non-immunogenic agents delivered via CFA may initiate low-grade persistent inflammation sufficient to produce articular damage over time. However, these findings refer primarily to experimental rodent models where arthritis manifests as an adverse effect following administrationnot necessarily spontaneous onset. No large-scale human trials have established causation between CFA exposure and human arthritis cases.
